pageTitle: Appealing to the congestion charging adjudicator

sections.heading: Appealing to the independent congestion charging adjudicator for a fine
sections.content:
If you have appealed to transport for London and they have refused your appeal then you can go to the independent congestion charging adjudicator. If you loose then you will have to pay the penalty charge at the full rate. Do not be put off by the loss of the 50% discount if you think you have a good case. Transport for London are delighted when you pay up.. Don’t let them get away with it if you think you have a good case.
Warning – do not ignore a congestion charge penalty charge notice as not only will you loose the discount and the right to appeal but you may also be issued with a charge certificate which increases the penalty. Also this the debt can be registered against you and eventually bailiffs can be instructed.
The procedure for the enforcement of a congestion charge penalty charge notice is similar to that for a parking ticket.
Next: Adjudicators Decisions on Congestion Charging
